untranslatable_reason: Pattern detection requires identifying two distinct peaks separated by time with amplitude comparison. This requires multi-bar lookback pattern recognition (peak detection, inter-peak distance measurement, and comparative peak height analysis) which cannot be expressed as simple indicator comparisons. The signal requires: (1) locating left peak high, (2) locating right peak high, (3) measuring days between peaks (51-55 days typical), (4) comparing peak heights (0-3% differential). Standard indicators cannot identify and compare discrete peak structures across variable time windows.
translation_notes: BULKOWSKI_DRY_0 (Equal Amplitude Double Top) is a structural price pattern that requires peak detection logic beyond single-bar indicator evaluation. Entry conditions describe peak morphology and temporal separation that need pattern recognition algorithms. Exit conditions marked AUTHOR_SILENT; applied reversal-based exit using price crossing above previous highs. The 72% success rate in downtrends and 19% average decline suggest a SHORT bias. Parameters like peak separation (51-55 days, up to 1 year) and peak height differential (0-3%) cannot be encoded in the rule format without pattern detection capabilities.
